methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $240M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Focused Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth that has raised $240M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The stage-progression method is applicable here. Using total capital raised of $240M as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $480M. The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints at the same stage and valuation tier within the nuclear energy subsector; while 8 nuclear peers exist, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage comp were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum needed for robust median calibration.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $408M to $552M, centered on $480M. This range reflects the 2.0x progression methodology with conservative and upside bounds of ±15%. The primary uncertainty stems from limited peer comparability at this stage within nuclear energy, a sector where financing velocity and capital requirements vary widely. Any refinement of this range would require either tighter same-stage peer selection within nuclear or validation through revenue multiples, capex benchmarking, or customer contract values—none of which are available in the current dataset.
